channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: YouTube TV price hike got you down? 5 free alternatives
item.description: Was YouTube TV’s recent price increase the straw that broke the camel’s back for you? When does the cost of streaming TV stop going up? When?!! The answer is: Never. These streaming services are going to keep creeping upward in cost until we’re all shelling out $300 a month. Enough is enough.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: AI use cases are going to get even bigger in 2025
item.description: Over the past two years, generative AI has dominated tech conversations and media headlines. Tools like ChatGPT, Gemini, Midjourney, and Sora captured imaginations with their ability to create text, images, and videos, sparking both excitement and ethical debates.
item.dc:creator: Kolawole Samuel Adebayo, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok is full of bogus, potentially dangerous medical advice
item.description: TikTok is the new doctor’s office, quickly becoming a go-to platform for medical advice. Unfortunately, much of that advice is sketchy at best. A new report by the healthcare software firm Tebra found 45% of medical advice on TikTok to be false or misleading.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Navy confronting hurdles to implementing 5G-enabled technologies at scale
item.description: 5G makes exciting emerging technologies possible, but first the Department of the Navy has to solve spectrum integration, cybersecurity and acquisition hurdles. The post Navy confronting hurdles to implementing 5G-enabled technologies at scale first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: The M4 MacBook Air is tipped to show up before new iPads and the iPhone SE 4 in 2025
item.description: We think we might know when the rumored M4 MacBook Air, the iPad 11, the iPad Air 7, and the iPhone SE 4 are showing up.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Scammers exploited mom’s fears to steal her entire life's savings
item.description: Rising elder scams: How to recognize them and protect yourself and your loved ones. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you outsmart the scammers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SAP customers still slow to deploy AI broadly
item.description: A new survey of SAP customer organizations shows that, despite AI experimentation, few have implemented AI and generative AI technologies across their enterprises.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Requisitos que deben cumplir los líderes de TI para navegar en la era de disrupción actual
item.description: Para su Informe sobre la preparación para la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa, la compañía de servicios de TI Avanade encuestó a más de 3.000 ejecutivos de TI y empresas de 10 países con, al menos, 500 millones de dólares en ingresos anuales.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump pushes back on 'President Musk' claims
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday rejected the recent dubbing of tech billionaire Elon Musk as “President Musk” by Democrats. “No, he’s not gonna be president, that I can tell you,” Trump said at conservative group Turning Point's “America Fest” in Phoenix, Ariz.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: This new AI chatbot is your personal present-finding wizard
item.description: Finding the perfect present isn’t always easy—especially if the people in your life are, let’s say, challenging to shop for. And while gift guides can be a great resource for certain purposes, they aren’t exactly catered to your personal needs and the tastes of those around you.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: House Republicans brush aside Musk's primary threats
item.description: House Republicans are largely brushing aside Elon Musk’s threats to unseat members who supported the initial government funding measure he helped torpedo earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The future belongs to systems of action
item.description: The world of enterprise tech is built on sturdy foundations. For decades, systems of record—the databases, customer relationship management (CRM), and enterprise resource planning (ERP) platforms we’re all intimately familiar with—have served as reliable pillars underpinning every major business.
item.dc:creator: John Sabino, Fast Company

item.title: Ex-OpenAI engineer who raised legal concerns about the technology he helped build has died
item.description: Suchir Balaji, a former OpenAI engineer and whistleblower who helped train the artificial intelligence systems behind ChatGPT and later said he believed those practices violated copyright law, has died, according to his parents and San Francisco officials.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple facilities. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union continued a strike Friday at seven of the company's delivery hubs just days before Christmas. At midnight on Saturday, workers at a prominent unionized warehouse in New York will also join, the Teamsters said.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New iPhone auto-restart feature locks encrypted data after extended inactivity
item.description: Apple's iOS 18.1 Inactivity Reboot automatically reboots your iPhone if it hasn't been used or unlocked for more than three days, providing better data protection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scholz dismisses Musk's assertion that only a far-right party can 'save' Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz on Friday dismissed an assertion by Elon Musk that only a far-right party can “save Germany,” but said that freedom of opinion "also goes for multibillionaires.” Germany is expected to vote in an early election on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to perform a clean install in Windows 10 and 11
item.description: One key technique in the Windows repair playbook involves wiping out everything on the storage device (typically C:\) from which Windows boots and on which that OS resides. Prosaically enough, this device is often called the boot/system drive or disk.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cost-conscious repatriation strategies
item.description: In recent years, the cloud computing landscape has undergone a seismic shift. After fully embracing the potential of public cloud services, many companies are reevaluating their strategies and shifting workloads back to on-premises infrastructures.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10 things you should never tell an AI chatbot
item.description: There are currently no laws governing what artificial intelligence can and cannot do with the information it gathers; here are 10 things to avoid telling AI chatbots to keep yourself safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters union to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: New app reveals political affiliations to US homebuyers
item.description: The platform believes social factors like age, income, and education influence buyers' decisions over home layouts.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: If Meta prevails against Apple in Europe, AI surveillance will be a feature, not a bug
item.description: Anyone using any digital device needs to wise up to a looming threat as Meta attempts to exploit the Europe Union’s continued assault against the Apple ecosystem to launch what seems to be an open season on your privacy.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australian who falsely claimed to have invented bitcoin is found in contempt of UK court
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An Australian computer scientist who falsely claimed to be the founder of the bitcoin cryptocurrency was found Thursday to be in contempt of an order of London's High Court and was sentenced to 12 months in prison, suspended for two years.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk considers funding Nigel Farage's populist party, a move that could shake up UK politics
item.description: LONDON (AP) — It’s a photo that sent a tremor through British politics: Elon Musk flanked by British politician Nigel Farage and a wealthy backer, in front of a gilt-framed painting of a young Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Apple complains Meta requests risk privacy in spat over EU efforts to widen access to iPhone tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple complained that requests from Meta Platforms for access to its operating software threaten user privacy, in a spat fueled by the European Union's intensifying efforts to get the iPhone maker to open up to products from tech rivals.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Getting started with Azure Managed Redis
item.description: Microsoft has made a lot of big bets in its preferred cloud-native infrastructure. You only need to look at .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Data on animal movements help Hungarian researchers create a swarm of autonomous drones
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Moving in a dense cloud, like throngs of people walking across a crowded public square, 100 drones maneuver through the night sky in Hungary's capital, the result of over a decade of research and experimentation that scientists believe could change the future of unmanned fli
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GitHub launches free tier of Copilot AI coding assistant
item.description: GitHub has launched GitHub Copilot Free, providing access to a free tier of the AI-based pair programmer via Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code editor. Announced December 18, Copilot Free gives developers access to 2,000 code completions and 50 chat messages monthly.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Slack adds Agentforce ‘hub’ for AI agents
item.description: Slack is adding a new way to access AI agents created on parent company Salesforce’s Agentforce platform, with a library of options available in the collaboration app. Agentforce, which launched in October, is a tool to build AI agents that answer questions and automate tasks for users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How the Trump administration is likely to deal with artificial intelligence
item.description: The National Defense Authorization Act for 2025 has extensive language on ensuring competition in AI. What can we expect from the Trump administration? The post How the Trump administration is likely to deal with artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Barcelona subway recycles energy from braking into power to charge electric cars
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — The Barcelona subway grinds to a halt, the doors slide open and commuters pour out to go about their daily business. Little do they know that as they do so, a burst of energy is sent up to street level to help charge an electric car.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, ABC 4
